channel.title: SevenAtSevenPolitics
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from SevenAtSevenPolitics, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Grandmother tells Trump critics to 'get off the man's back' during White House Black History Month event
item.description: A woman who lost her grandson to gun violence defended President Trump at this year's White House Black History Month event, telling critics to "get o...

item.title: Davis: If GOP Wants To Win The Midterms, They Need To Pass The SAVE Act
item.description: 'So far this year, I think the Senate has voted on average once a day. They have passed two bills. Why not bring up the SAVE America Act?'

item.title: Elon Musk is pushing to build data centers in space. But they won’t solve AI’s power problems anytime soon
item.description: Proponents say orbital data centers could solve AI’s power and cooling crunch. Experts say that vision is far more distant than the hype suggests.

item.title: Top defense contractor announces major move weeks after Dem governor takes office: 'Win for the heartland'
item.description: Boeing announced it is moving its defense headquarters out of Virginia back to St. Louis, just weeks after newly elected Democratic Gov. Abigail Spanb...

item.title: Adversaries: California plan to tax billionaires puts Sanders and Newsom at odds
item.description: As national Democrats search for a unifying theme ahead of the fall's midterm elections, a California proposal to levy a hefty tax on billionaires is ...

item.title: Bill Gates pulls out of India’s AI summit at the last minute in the latest blow to an event dogged by organizational chaos
item.description: The Microsoft founder's abrupt withdrawal, amid renewed scrutiny over his ties to Jeffrey Epstein, capped a chaotic few days for an event already dogg...

item.title: Despite a $400 million net worth and Hollywood career, Reese Witherspoon tells young people to stop chasing their dreams—and do this instead
item.description: Reese Witherspoon is telling young people to stop romanticizing the “dream job” and start getting brutally honest about what they’re actually good at.

item.title: Google CEO Sundar Pichai says AI spending still makes sense despite bubble fears
item.description: The Google CEO touched down in New Delhi this week with a string of new India investments in tow.

item.title: Trump hits campaign trail in key battleground as race to replace Marjorie Taylor Greene heats up
item.description: Trump visits Georgia to showcase economic efforts and policy agenda as special election looms in Greene's district amid midterm battleground politics.

item.title: DHS shutdown leaves local emergency responders on their own amid extreme weather, expert warns
item.description: A public safety expert warned that the DHS shutdown could devastate local disaster response, with grant funding impacted as extreme weather threatens ...

item.title: Who is OpenClaw creator Peter Steinberger? The millennial developer caught the attention of Sam Altman and Mark Zuckerberg
item.description: Steinberger made $100M off his first company and quit coding. Now he’s back to show the world the “magic” of agentic AI.

item.title: Strapped New Yorkers swarm chaotic Mamdani-inspired free grocery store pop-up: We’re 'in pain’
item.description: NYC residents face grocery costs of $400 to $500 monthly as Polymarket opens free grocery pop-up in West Village, highlighting the city's affordabilit...

item.title: Republicans sue to stop Virginia redistricting referendum
item.description: Republican organizations filed a lawsuit Wednesday objecting to Virginia Democrats' 2026 redistricting ballot referendum for using misleading language...

item.title: Abolish the FCC’s Equal-Time Rule
item.description: Government should not be able to decide which political candidates must and must not appear on broadcast television.

item.title: NC Election Board Agrees To Fix Thousands Of Voter Registrations Lacking Required ID Numbers
item.description: The North Carolina State Election Board agreed to no longer register voters missing required information.

item.title: Norman C. Francis, civil rights champion and recipient of Presidential Medal of Freedom, dies at 94
item.description: Norman C. Francis, a civil rights pioneer and champion of education who played a pivotal role in helping rebuild New Orleans after Hurricane Katrina, ...

item.title: FDA will drop two-study requirement for new drug approvals, aiming to speed access
item.description: The Food and Drug Administration plans to drop its longtime standard of requiring two rigorous studies to win approval for new drugs, the latest chang...

item.title: Following Munich, the U.S.–European Relationship Faces a Key Moment
item.description: In the face of EU chest-puffing, it shouldn’t be surprising that the U.S. is willing to take a fighting stance in Europe.

item.title: Stephen Colbert’s Talarico Tantrum Further Vindicates CBS
item.description: Is it any surprise his show is getting canceled?

item.title: Stephen Colbert’s Nightly Democrat Extravaganza Collides With FCC’s ‘Equal Time’ Rule
item.description: The crybaby late-night host insists he's a victim of political pressure, but he's been a long-time abuser of the nation's broadcast airwaves.

item.title: Dems Rig Virginia Vote With Misleading Ballot Language Equating Gerrymandering Push To ‘Fairness’
item.description: Some Virginians have noticed what appears to be a lack of institutional effort by Republicans in battling the referendum.

item.title: WH adviser Hassett urges 'discipline' for Fed economists over tariff study
item.description: President Donald Trump's top economist on Wednesday urged that Federal Reserve economists be punished for research last week that showed American comp...

item.title: The Interest Rate Panic Is Historically Illiterate
item.description: Outside of periods of global catastrophe, rates have long been considerably higher than they are now.

item.title: NYT Accidentally Confesses There’s A Left-Wing Judicial Coup Against Trump
item.description: As admitted by Toobin, Democrat-appointed judges have become a critical feature of the left's evolving lawfare against Trump and his voters.

item.title: Should Justice Alito Retire? It’s Complicated
item.description: Politics alone suggests that one or both of the most senior justices should retire. But there’s always more to that decision.

item.title: Attention Bias in AI-Driven Investing
item.description: AI tools may favor popular stocks over overlooked ones, embedding attention bias into investment decisions. The post Attention Bias in AI-Driven Inves...

item.title: Social Security Claiming Strategies for High-Net-Worth Clients
item.description: Social Security claiming is a capital-allocation decision for affluent clients. This analysis weighs taxes, longevity risk, and liquidity trade-offs. ...

item.title: Evolving Your Wealth Management Practice for 2026 and Beyond
item.description: Wealth management’s 2026 outlook: growth will hinge on transparency, integration, and relevance to women and next-generation investors. The post Evolv...

item.title: AI Is Reshaping Bank Risk
item.description: AI is exposing the limits of legacy bank controls, making governance quality critical to resilience and investor confidence. The post AI Is Reshaping ...

item.title: What Makes an Ideal Leveraged Buyout Candidate?
item.description: In competitive PE markets, successful LBOs depend on durable business models, recurring cash flows, defensible market positions, and disciplined targe...
